J.B. in the house, time for your daily motivation slash skills. Today's about the skill. The skill is goal setting. Listen, I really dissect this in the certification program and I just want those of you on certification to make sure, to make sure that you really get this, that you really have actually done the actual, the outer world. I mean, I gave you the information, I gave you the blueprint, right? I want to make sure that you fully act on this now. There are a few steps in particular of this eight step process that I want to go through and sort of just dissect even more just to make sure you get this right because once you got this, remember, you know, your goals are not always meant to be hit. You want to stretch when you set goals. The old joke I made, say to everybody in a big room, okay, everybody raise your hand as high as you possibly can. I mean, as high as you possibly can. And they all like this, like, no, no, just a little bit high. Like, I'm like, what the hell's that about? I mean, I just said as high as you possibly can, but you had this, that's called stretch. That's your stretch point. See, what I'm not telling you to do when you set goals is like, you know, I want to go out there and be the first person to mine gold on an asteroid outside of Jupiter between Uranus. I mean, okay. I mean, maybe if you were Elon Musk, I'd give you the benefit of that. But if you're an average person, that's not really a goal. Your brain says what? Bullshit. Bullshit. It's got no power. But likewise, if you set your goals too low, too weakly, right? And you say, I want to be like, you know, I want to make 100 grand next year when you're making 94 right now. You know, that's not, you know, you'll do, you'll hit that goal and you'll be average. And you won't have the impetus to change. You'd be like, everything's all right. Hit my goal. I feel okay. Right. And there's no impetus to change. That's the problem when you set your goals too low. The enemy of great isn't awful. The enemy of great is what? Good. You're good enough. That's not what this is about. The straight line has never been about good enough. And certainly straight line certification is not a good enough program. It's got a good enough factor, which means you get results straight away. And you guys have seen that already. All right. You can see how quickly this stuff ramps up your closing rate. All right. That's just scratching the surface. All right. Scratching the surface. Even my own team here, my head of sales, Matt, as I work with Matt and train Matt and show Matt the deeper levels of the straight line, I watch him become infinitely more proficient. It's a good enough factor, but then there's this level of excellence and amazingness and extraordinary, extraordinaryness. Sometimes I only have a word. I almost said the F bottom. But I pulled it back just in time. Anyway, the point I'm making here is that you've got to set lofty goals, but they can't be bullshit. All right. Now let's talk about a couple of steps. You know my eight steps, right? You know, set a goal, put a date on, identify the groups and people and the organizations that you can align yourself with. Okay. What people do you need to know? What skills do you have to learn? Blah, blah, blah. Develop a plan and here's where I want to stop. Develop a plan. The place that most people fall down here is in the planning stage. Either they think they have to have the perfect plan and they never actually take the next step, which is what? Take action on that plan. Take massive action, massive directed action, meaning on plan, either they are perpetually waiting to develop the perfect plan or B, they have developed a plan that is God dawn awful because they tried to reinvent the wheel, meaning that plan is just drawn up from the top of the head. What am I driving at here? I'm driving at something called modeling. Modeling is the secret to tying together the outer world of success with the inner world. Vision is an inner world competency and vision focus that two step process, you create a vision and learn how to focus on that with that bifurcated focus rather than focusing on your fears and the obstacles and the challenges. That's what this is all about. What really ties this together and makes it actually usable is modeling, meaning developing plans, strategies that are based on already proven formulas. You're going through the straight line certification for a reason. Why? You're modeling the world's most effective system of sales and influence. It is. It's a shortcut. You know it works. You say, okay, let me put the guys at this master's success is train more people than anybody else in the world. He's created more top producers than anybody else in the world. Let me just do it his way and adopt it, take that and adopt it and modify it into my own business versus let me just sell the way I want to sell because it's without having a model. I'm not saying mine is the only model. That's my father best one. I promise you that. There are other models as well, but if you choose no model, good luck. Friend of mine Ed in my leg teaches entrepreneurs. Ed's a really first class entrepreneur. He teaches that. I teach that too, but he teaches it well. He teaches it really well. If I was an entrepreneur, I would look at his stuff, develop a model. If I was advertising on Facebook, marketing, I would go to the best marketing. I do that by the way. When I was first getting started, I used to get an Alex Merle, one of my early mentors, show me the ropes. Now I have someone else once at the next level, so it's doing more corporate stuff for me. Point is you model. You don't invent plans. The plan matters. You can't wait for the perfect plan. You can't. And you can't wing it. So that's my question. What is that magical sweet spot? That's the million dollar question. So along with modeling, there's this other counterpart to that, which is called mentorship, meaning having someone that's been there, done it at the highest level, knows your business, that's willing to talk to you and help you out. Well, that's the counterpart to start with a model that you're meant to help you out. Does that make sense? And then you take action. That's key. That's where most people fall down, is they don't reconcile the inner and the outer world. They might possess the individual skills. They might have mastered the straight line. They might have mastered marketing even, you know, in some way, right? Maybe not. Doesn't matter. But they don't have the plan. They haven't modeled an industry-specific plan that sets them up for success. Just the mistake people make. And they try to change their approach and change their approach, but they start off with a flawed perspective. So remember, modeling and mentorship, the two M's, leads to massive success. That's the lesson for today. And remember, you combine that with this amazing vision, you can't go wrong. All right, we'll talk in tomorrow.
